name: mTOR inhibitor review frames Rapalogs as translational but not settled human geroprotection
summary: The Nature Aging review supports Rapalogs as a central translational geroscience branch while keeping boundaries around dose, selectivity, chronic safety, clinical endpoints, and the gap between animal longevity and standard-of-care human use.
statement: A 2023 Nature Aging review frames rapamycin and mTOR inhibitors as strong aging-biology and translational candidates, reviews animal longevity and early clinical trials, and emphasizes unresolved questions about safer mTORC1-selective inhibition, aging-related indications, and the work needed before mTOR inhibitors become standard care for diseases of aging.
population_or_model: mTOR inhibitor aging literature across models and human trials
caveats: Review-level synthesis is not a new efficacy result.
The review reinforces translational plausibility while preserving safety, selectivity, dose, and endpoint uncertainty.
mTOR pathway evidence overlaps immune rejuvenation, autophagy, metabolism, senescence, and disease-specific claims.
